title: pavonia undulata
intro: Pavonia undulata, also known as the rock rose or rose mallow, is a plant species belonging to the mallow family and is native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. The medicinal properties of Pavonia undulata include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other compounds. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and help with menstrual cramps due to its emmenagogic properties, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of digestive issues and wounds. Herbal preparations of Pavonia undulata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
